name: Garlic Bubble Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Mix well.
2. In a saucepan, heat milk, water, and shortening until warm (shortening does not need to melt). Add this mixture to the flour mixture. Stir in the egg. Blend at low speed until moistened; beat for 3 minutes at medium speed.
3. With clean hands, gradually stir in the remaining flour on a floured surface until a soft dough forms. Knead for 25-30 strokes.
4. Divide the dough into 12 pieces and shape into balls. In a bowl, combine melted butter, paprika, and garlic powder. Mix well.
5. Dip the dough balls into the butter mixture and place in a greased 9x5-inch loaf pan (nine inches by five inches), using six balls per layer. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
6. Cover the pan and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 45 minutes.
7. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake for 40-45 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from the pan and serve warm. This is a one-rise, garlic bread in a pull-apart bubble loaf. Makes 1 loaf.
